judgments.reasoning: gddp_verifier.ts registers submit_verdict via pi.registerTool() with SubmitVerdictParams that mirrors SemanticOutput (judgments, overall_reasoning, risks, followup_candidates, budget_exhausted, budget_trace). The JudgmentSchema matches CriterionJudgment. execute() reads GDDP_VERDICT_OUT from process.env, validates args, writes JSON via writeFileSync, and returns terminate:true in all paths.

evidence: scripts/runtime/verification/semantic/pi_runner.py:99-117 (PiHarnessRunner.run() builds command, spawns subprocess, reads verdict)
scripts/runtime/verification/semantic/pi_runner.py:102-109 (sets GDDP_VERDICT_OUT env var; cwd=str(repo) on subprocess)
judgments.reasoning: PiHarnessRunner.run() spawns pi via subprocess.run with --print --mode text, loads both gddp_verifier.ts and guard extensions via -e, uses PI_SYSTEM_PROMPT (a pi-specific system prompt with GDDP investigator instructions), sets cwd=str(repo) so pi operates in the target repo, reads the verdict file from GDDP_VERDICT_OUT path, validates via SemanticOutput.model_validate(), and returns SemanticOutput. build_prompt_messages() is reused for consistency.

evidence: scripts/runtime/verification/orchestrator.py:37-40 (if semantic_harness is not None: semantic = semantic_harness(...))
judgments.reasoning: orchestrator.verify() accepts optional semantic_harness callable typed as SemanticHarness (Callable[..., SemanticOutput]). When set, it replaces SemanticAgent.run() entirely. When None (default), the original SemanticAgent path is used unchanged. The CLI already uses this hook by passing PiHarnessRunner.run when --semantic-harness pi is selected. All existing orchestrator tests pass unchanged.

semantic.overall_reasoning:
The pi-evaluator-harness node successfully wires the pi coding agent as a live, streaming semantic verifier while preserving every GDDP contract. 

Criteria 1-4 (pi-extension-registers-submit-verdict, pi-runner-drives-pi, orchestrator-harness-hook, cli-harness-flag) are all clearly satisfied at high confidence through direct source code inspection. The gddp_verifier.ts extension properly registers a typed submit_verdict terminal tool matching SemanticOutput; PiHarnessRunner spawns pi with --print --mode text, the correct extension, PI_SYSTEM_PROMPT, and cwd=repo; orchestrator.verify() accepts an optional semantic_harness callable with backward-compatible default; and cli.py exposes --semantic-harness {auto,pi,runner} (auto→runner), --semantic-thinking, and --semantic-pi-model.

Criterion 5 (live-run-produces-valid-receipt) is indeterminate at 0.7 confidence. The code paths are structurally correct and handoff evidence confirms live runs against vault-doctor/scan-vault-core produced valid receipts. A persistent self-referential receipt exists proving the contract works. However, no persistent vault-doctor-specific receipt artifact exists for independent verification.

Criterion 6 (suite-green) is indeterminate at 0.6 confidence. The live self-referential run confirms 67 tests pass, but the current verification environment lacks pytest and pydantic, preventing independent test execution.

All five constraints are respected: (1) decision_engine matrix (12 rows) and VerdictReceipt contract unchanged; (2) OpenAICompatibleRunner (line 67) and AnthropicRunner (line 33) remain in agent.py; (3) the guard extension hard-blocks edit/write/multi_edit and dangerous bash; (4) PiHarnessRunner is model-agnostic via provider/model parameters; (5) no graph mutations — the code only produces evidence.

The required artifacts (decision.md, result-summary.md, patch.diff) exist in .gddp/ (from prior node, not this node's artifacts). Per semantic verification instructions, artifact completeness is a separate concern from criteria satisfaction.
semantic.risks: 1. Criterion 5 (live-run) is untestable from this environment - a pi binary with API keys is required for full end-to-end validation. A regression could break the harness silently. 2. No unit tests exist specifically for PiHarnessRunner or the CLI --semantic-harness pi path; test coverage relies on the existing 67 test suite. 3. The self-referential receipt in gddp-config was generated by a live pi run against this very node, creating a circular dependency that may confuse future verification. 4. Required artifacts (decision.md, result-summary.md, patch.diff) exist but are from a prior node (verification-receipt-contract), not from this node's execution.

Add unit tests for PiHarnessRunner (mock pi binary, test command-line construction, verdict file parsing, error handling). 3. Add CLI tests for --semantic-harness pi, --semantic-thinking, and --semantic-pi-model flags. 4. Generate this node's own required artifacts (decision.md, result-summary.md, patch.diff). 5. Note: the test suite (criterion 6) could not be independently verified due to missing pytest/pydantic in this environment. A dedicated test runner environment would resolve this.

The pi-evaluator-harness node successfully wires the pi coding agent as a live, streaming semantic verifier while preserving every GDDP contract. 

Criteria 1-4 (pi-extension-registers-submit-verdict, pi-runner-drives-pi, orchestrator-harness-hook, cli-harness-flag) are all clearly satisfied at high confidence through direct source code inspection. The gddp_verifier.ts extension properly registers a typed submit_verdict terminal tool matching SemanticOutput; PiHarnessRunner spawns pi with --print --mode text, the correct extension, PI_SYSTEM_PROMPT, and cwd=repo; orchestrator.verify() accepts an optional semantic_harness callable with backward-compatible default; and cli.py exposes --semantic-harness {auto,pi,runner} (auto→runner), --semantic-thinking, and --semantic-pi-model.

Criterion 5 (live-run-produces-valid-receipt) is indeterminate at 0.7 confidence. The code paths are structurally correct and handoff evidence confirms live runs against vault-doctor/scan-vault-core produced valid receipts. A persistent self-referential receipt exists proving the contract works. However, no persistent vault-doctor-specific receipt artifact exists for independent verification.

Criterion 6 (suite-green) is indeterminate at 0.6 confidence. The live self-referential run confirms 67 tests pass, but the current verification environment lacks pytest and pydantic, preventing independent test execution.

All five constraints are respected: (1) decision_engine matrix (12 rows) and VerdictReceipt contract unchanged; (2) OpenAICompatibleRunner (line 67) and AnthropicRunner (line 33) remain in agent.py; (3) the guard extension hard-blocks edit/write/multi_edit and dangerous bash; (4) PiHarnessRunner is model-agnostic via provider/model parameters; (5) no graph mutations — the code only produces evidence.

The required artifacts (decision.md, result-summary.md, patch.diff) exist in .gddp/ (from prior node, not this node's artifacts). Per semantic verification instructions, artifact completeness is a separate concern from criteria satisfaction.
